mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress discount rates on equity cash flows, lifting valuations and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as the accommodation is priced in.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 6% signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in periods when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time, or average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong GDP growth and rising retail sales indicate robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings acceleration, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth > 2% and RSAFS YoY growth > 4%
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not yield an average forward 20-day S&P 500 return significantly above the unconditional average (paired t-test p>0.10) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment with headline CPI inflation above 3%, a rising federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases recession probability and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increase of at least 25bp over 3 months
failure_condition: If in periods with CPI YoY > 3%, a rising FEDFUNDS is not followed by a negative median forward 20-day S&P 500 return (sign test p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and real economic output, which supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 total returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return has a negative rank correlation with INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window (Spearman rho < 0).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic contraction, leading to negative investor sentiment and lower S&P 500 prices in the following month.
failure_condition: If a 10% drop in housing starts is followed by positive forward 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains combined with robust retail sales growth confirm a virtuous cycle of employment and consumption, boosting profit expectations and generating positive short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200K and RSAFS monthly change > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce a positive mean forward 5-day S&P 500 return with an annualized Sharpe ratio greater than 0.5 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment already at or below 5%, a sudden increase in the unemployment rate signals that the economy is tipping into recession, prompting negative equity repricing over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 30bp over 1 month
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE <= 5%, a monthly rise of 30bp is not followed by a negative average 20-day forward S&P 500 return (one-sided t-test p>0.10).
